methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
LeydenJar Technologies is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Christian Rood and Gabriël de Scheemaker that has raised €50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of six same-stage, same-sector companies, supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA) and eight additional private peers in climate and energy technology.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The median valuation across the six direct comparables—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M)—is $355M. This method establishes a reference point, though the peer range spans $120M to $5.0B, reflecting meaningful dispersion around technology risk, capital intensity, and market maturity within the sector at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $302M to $408M, centered on the peer median of $355M. Key uncertainties include the broad dispersion within the Series B climate-tech cohort, which reflects heterogeneity in technology readiness and capital requirements, and the limited applicability of revenue or earnings multiples given pre-commercial or early-revenue profiles typical at this stage. The median estimate should be treated as indicative of market positioning relative to contemporaneous peers rather than a precise valuation.
